rulings.Case Title: QUEEN ESTHER PIERCE et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, AUGUST 15, 2008, LINE 6, DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. DECEDENT'S TESTIMONY IS ADMISSIBLE FOR DEFENDANT HAD THE OPPORTUNITY TO DEPOSE DECEDENT AND CHOSE NOT TO. DEFENDANT FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE THAT PLAINTIFF IS UNABLE TO ESTABLISH LIABILITY BASED ON EXPOSURE FROM PLANT'S WORK. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: QUEEN ESTHER PIERCE et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, AUGUST 15, 2008, LINE 6, DEFENDANT CERTAINTEED CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO CLAIM FOR FALSE REPRESENTATION. DENIED AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES, FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. PLAINTIFFS RESPONSE TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY 52 IS NOT FACTUALLY DEVOID. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: INITA MIGLENIECE VS. RUBICON, A BUSINESS ORGANIZATION, FORM UNKNOWN et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, AUGUST 15, 2008, LINE 8, DEFENDANTS PIMENTO, INC., D.A.N. MYRIAD HOSPITALITY CORP., MYRIAD RESTAURANT GROUP, INC., PIMENTO INVESTORS, AND MYRIAD RESTAURANT GROUP'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO SEPTEMBER 30, 2008. MOVANTS ARE CORPORATE ENTITIES AND ON JULY 2, 2008, THEIR LAWYERS WITHDREW. MOVANTS TO OBTAIN NEW COUNSEL BECAUSE A CORPORATION CANNOT REPRESENT ITSELF IN PRO PER. SEE MERCO CONST. ENGINEERS V. MUNI CRT., (1978) 21 CAL.3D. 724. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: INITA MIGLENIECE VS. RUBICON, A BUSINESS ORGANIZATION, FORM UNKNOWN et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, AUGUST 15, 2008, LINE 8, DEFENDANT PIMENTO, INC., D.A.N. MYRIAD HOSPITALITY CORP., MYRIAD RESTAURANT GROUP, INC., PIMENTO INVESTORS, AND MYRIAD RESTAURANT GROUP'S MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO SEPTEMBER 30, 2008. MOVANTS ARE CORPORATE ENTITIES AND ON JULY 2, 2008, THEIR LAWYERS WITHDREW. MOVANTS TO OBTAIN NEW COUNSEL BECAUSE A CORPORATION CANNOT REPRESENT ITSELF IN PRO PER. SEE MERCO CONST. ENGINEERS V. MUNI CRT., (1978) 21 CAL.3D. 724. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: CASSANDRA C GRAPSA-APOSTOLIDOU VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, A et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, AUGUST 15, 2008, LINE 9, DEFENDANT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 8TH CAUSE OF ACTION, THE COURT DID NOT GRANT LEAVE TO ADD ADDITIONAL CAUSES OF ACTIONS. FURTHER AS TO THE 7TH AND 8TH CAUSES OF ACTION, THE FACTS ALLEGED DO NOT SUPPORT A CLAIM FOR MEDICAL BATTERY UNDER COBBS V. GRANT (1972) 8 CAL.3D 299, 239. PLAINTIFF CONSENTED TO THE PROCEDURE AND WAS CONSCIOUS OF BEING GIVEN INJECTIONS. THE 9TH AND 10TH CAUSES DO NOT ALLEGE EXTREME AND OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT AS CONTRASTED A CLAIM FOR DENTAL MALPRACTICE. THE FACTS ALLEGED IN THE 11TH CAUSE OF ACTION ARE PRIVILEGED UNDER SHOEMAKER V. FRIEDBERG (1947) 80 CAL.APP.2D 911, 915-916. THE 14TH CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS TO ALLEGE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: JUAN CESPEDES et al VS. CENTERSTONE REALTY, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Peition To Compel Mediation/Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, AUGUST 15, 2008, LINE 10, DEFENDANT DAWN WIGGINS Petition To Compel Mediation/Arbitration. THE MOTION IS DENIED. NEITHER CENTERSTONE NOR ERICKSON ARE PARTIES TO THE AGREEMENT. AS A CONSEQUENCE, THERE IS A RISK OF INCONSISTENT AND CONFLICTING RULINGS. CCP SECTION 1281.2. =(302/PJM/PB)
